howItWorks.intro: This income tax calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Tax is calculated progressively through each bracket
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How do tax brackets work?
faq.answer: The US uses a progressive system where only income within each bracket is taxed at that rate. A $75K single filer pays 10% on the first $11,600, 12% on $11,601-$47,150, and 22% on $47,151-$75,000.

faq.question: What is the difference between marginal and effective rate?
faq.answer: Marginal rate is the bracket your last dollar falls in. Effective rate is total tax divided by total income — always lower than your marginal rate.

faq.question: Single vs married filing jointly?
faq.answer: Married filing jointly has wider brackets and higher standard deduction, generally resulting in lower taxes. However, two high earners may face a marriage penalty.

faq.question: Does this include state taxes?
faq.answer: No, this calculates federal income tax only. Add your state tax rate separately. Nine states have no income tax.
